name: Bourke's Luck Potholes
description: Raging waters have created a strange geological site that looks like something off of a progressive rock album cover.

name: Boulders Beach Penguin Sanctuary
description: This African beach is home to thousands of rare mainland penguins that are known for their annoying voice.

name: Bo Kaap
description: Formerly known as the Malay Quarter, Bo Kaap is a colorfully-painted suburb located in the “Mother City” of South Africa.

name: Adam's Calendar
description: Hidden in the hills is a mysterious stone structure, known only to a few.

name: South African Astronomical Observatory
description: This historic observatory was established to keep shipwrecks from littering the Cape of Good Hope.

name: Collectors Treasury
description: Over a million items cram the shelves at Africa's largest shop of used and rare books.

name: Tapi Tapi Ice Cream
description: The frozen treats at this Cape Town parlor are inspired by Africa's food cultures.

name: Seal Island
description: Haven for Cape Fur Seals, until the Ring of Death of jumping Great White Sharks arrives.

name: Sudwala Caves
description: These otherworldly caverns form Earth's oldest known cave system.

name: Cango Caves
description: Largest cave system in Africa.

name: Neighbourgoods Market
description: A growing popular hipster market with over 100 local specialties and live music.

name: District Six Museum
description: A sobering account of the vibrant multicultural neighborhood that was destroyed under apartheid.

name: Ponte Tower
description: The tallest residential building in Africa is an unsettling Brutalist monolith.

name: Howick Falls
description: Local lore says a serpent-like beast lurks in the deep basin below the falls.

name: Chapman's Peak Drive
description: This road winds through the steep coastal cliffside offering spectacular views for those who brave the drive.

name: Sun City Resort
description: A safari-themed castle in the South African bush with the largest maze in the southern hemisphere.

name: Shark Cage Diving in Gansbaai
description: An odd little fishing town that is inundated by tourists looking to be locked up in a cage and surrounded by great white sharks, because why not?

name: Sterkfontein Caves
description: Caves that can't seem to quit producing the remains of prehistoric humans.

name: Cradle of Humankind
description: Home to some of the oldest hominid fossils ever found.

name: The Big Pineapple
description: The world's largest pineapple building honors Bathurst's favorite fruit.

name: Tswaing Meteorite Crater
description: An ancient impact crater on the outskirts of Pretoria.

name: The Goat Tower of Fairview Cheese & Wine Farm
description: The original goat tower, the one that started it all.

name: Big Hole and Open Mine Museum
description: Mined to a depth of 240 meters with a perimeter of about 1.6 km, the largest hand-dug excavation in the world.

name: Afrikaans Language Monument
description: The South African dialect's complex history is immortalized in stone, in one of the world's only monuments dedicated to a language.

name: Nelson Mandela Capture Site
description: A mesmerizing sculpture marks the site where the activist was arrested after evading capture for 17 months.

name: The Post Office Tree
description: South Africa's postal service began with a letter left in a boot beneath this tree.

name: Foreshore Freeway Bridge
description: Despite many attempts to finish this overpass, it has been abandoned since the 1970s.

name: Cape Agulhas Marker
description: This small monument marks the divide between the Atlantic and Indian Oceans at the southernmost tip of Africa.

name: Ronnie's Sex Shop
description: “The oddest pub in South Africa,” inspired by a practical joke.

name: Orlando Towers
description: Two giant painted cooling towers make for one of the world’s most epic bungee jumps.

name: Motouleng Caves
description: The subterranean sanctuary home to local shamans is a sacred site for people of all religions.

name: African Raptor Centre
description: Environmentally crucial raptors like eagles and vultures are kept and protected in this massive sanctuary.

name: Sani Pass
description: Brace yourself for the slowest, scariest, most spectacular drive, down the Drakensberg Escarpment.

name: Vredefort Impact Crater
description: The world's largest impact crater is mainly eroded but still remembers the planet's violent history.

name: Museum of Man and Science
description: A supply store for traditional healers, this shop sells the hair of a dog--and its skin, skeleton, and reproductive organs, too.

name: Hole-in-the-Wall
description: A massive rock in the surf steeped in legends.

name: Wildebeest Kuil Rock Art Centre
description: Fascinating collection of Khoisan rock engravings, still situated in their original setting.

name: Montecasino Bird Gardens
description: Over 60 species of birds inhabit this colorful aviary park in South Africa.

name: Wreck of the Meisho Maru No. 38
description: All that remains of a Japanese fishing vessel that ran aground in 1982.

name: Just Nuisance Statue
description: A memorial to the only dog ever officially enlisted in the Royal Navy.

name: Map of Africa
description: A beautiful viewpoint on the Garden Route overlooking a naturally-occurring outline of Africa.

name: Mzinyathi Falls
description: This beautiful waterfall is not easy to reach, but offers a stunning view of the pristine green valley.

name: Wood's Cycad
description: The loneliest plant in the world resides at the Durban Botanic Gardens...mostly.

name: Kaaimans Grotto
description: This cave overlooking the Indian Ocean has been turned into a shelter and makeshift museum.

name: Grand Central Water Tower
description: A giant concrete Brutalist water tower in suburban Johannesburg.

name: Klerksdorp Spheres
description: Tiny pyrophyllite spheres from 3 billion years ago.

name: Owl House
description: Sculpture park built by a 50-year-old widow.

name: Satyagraha House
description: The home of Mahatma Gandhi during part of the 21 years he spent living in South Africa is now a guest house.

name: Mandela House
description: Nelson Mandela lived here with his family from 1946 until his arrest in 1962.

name: Witwatersrand Basin
description: It's estimated that half of the world's gold was mined from South Africa's "golden arc."

name: Bird Island Nature Reserve
description: This small island off Lambert's Bay is home to more than 2,000 Cape gannets and is the only such breeding colony open to the public.

name: 'IiNkwenkwezi eziPhahlileyo' ('The Stars Beside')
description: Many generations ago, it was said that one could find celestial splendor in a lush meadow not far from this very marker according to the lore of the fictional world Kcymaerxthaere.

name: Huberta the Hippo
description: Taxidermy of a famous hippo who spent three years walking over a thousand miles across South Africa.

name: The Most Southwestern Point of Africa
description: The Cape of Good Hope is not quite as low as you can go on the African continent, but it's still geographically significant.

name: ‘The Shadow Boxer’ at Chancellor House
description: A large-scale steel sculpture entitled 'The Shadow Boxer' pays tribute to Nelson Mandela.

name: Donkergat Whaling Station
description: At one South African whaling station, this industrial extermination led to a feeding frenzy that may have even been a key inspiration for Jaws.

name: Liliesleaf Farm Museum
description: This unassuming farm complex was once the epicenter of an underground liberation group.

name: Red Desert
description: Is this 200-meter-wide nature reserve the smallest desert in the world?

name: The Noon Gun
description: From atop a hill at the center of Cape Town, a cannon has been signalling the time since 1806.

name: Wonderwerk Cave
description: A million years ago, our ancient ancestors were cooking with fire in this cave.

name: Heart of Cape Town Museum
description: The first successful human heart transplant is depicted through wax figures at the hospital where it the surgery took place.

name: The Sacramento Cannon Monument
description: Artillery recovered from the sea floor marks the spot of an extraordinary shipwreck.

name: Point of Human Origin
description: A cave that was home to humans 160 thousand years ago.

name: Sudwala Dinosaur Park
description: Jurassic Park in South Africa.

name: Ntaba ka Ndoda
description: A monument with a complex history dedicated to one of South Africa's greatest heroes.

name: Time Ball Tower
description: A 19th-century relic of timekeeping.

name: Scratch Patch
description: An artificial cave littered with semi-precious stones waiting to be picked up.

name: Gantouw Pass Wagon Trail
description: These 250-year-old wagon tracks are a lasting sign of Dutch migrants crossing the mountains.

name: Latimer's Landing
description: The spot where the first living specimen of the coelacanth was discovered.

name: Lydenburg Heads
description: These terracotta heads are among the oldest Iron Age objects found south of the equator.

name: Smokkelhuis
description: A vast and varied antique and vintage collection that borders on museum status.

name: Skull Garden
description: On the grounds of a game lodge, a path decorated with the skulls of all kinds of animals.

name: Vergelegen Camphor Trees
description: Planted more than 300 years ago, these trees are South African National Monuments.

name: Lisbon Falls
description: The tallest waterfall in the region is named after a capital far, far away.

name: Fernkloof Nature Reserve
description: The world’s most biodiverse place when it comes to plants.

name: Dylan Lewis Studio and Sculpture Garden
description: A sculpted indigenous garden featuring more than 60 works by Dylan Lewis.

name: Battle of Berg-en-dal Monument
description: A brutalist war memorial piercing through the rolling farm landscape.

name: World's Largest Aloe Statue
description: A larger-than-life representation of the humble aloe plant.

name: Tuinplaas - Tiny Town
description: A miniature park, fairy garden, milkshake bar, museum, and micronation in one.

name: Beach Hut Whale Bones
description: This arch made out of whale bones is a remnant of South Africa's whaling history.

name: Poolsmoor Prison Cantina
description: Breakfast and lunch in a canteen where the servers and kitchen staff are prison inmates.

name: The Model Shipyard
description: A custom shop making factory that produces models for collectors and movies.

name: Devonian Ecosystems Project
description: A fossil collection of the only known Devonian high-latitude estuarine ecosystem.

name: Drostdy Museum
description: A museum complex showing 17th-century life in Swellendam.

name: RMS Athens Shipwreck
description: The engine block of a British Royal Mail Ship marks the spot of her demise, caused by the Great Gale of 1856.

name: Janine Iron and Washing Museum
description: A large collection of irons and washing machines dedicated to the memory of a lost daughter.

name: Long March for Freedom
description: A collection of over 100 statues of people who fought for the freedom of South Africa.

name: Nobel Square
description: It celebrates the four men from South Africa who have won the Nobel Peace Prize.

name: Cecil Rhodes Plinth
description: The last remnant of a controversial statue at Cape Town University.

name: Sunland Baobab
description: A South African bar inside a hollowed out 6,000-year-old Baobab.

name: Macassar Beach Pavilion
description: An abandoned water park overtaken by the dunes.

name: BOS 400 Shipwreck
description: The haunting beauty of this little-visited wreck makes it a true hidden treasure.

name: Top of Africa
description: One of the tallest buildings in Africa provides a great view of Johannesburg's unique geography.
